name: Thai Coconut Chicken Soup
tagline: Tom kha gai — coconut, galangal, lemongrass, lime, fish sauce
highlight: Fish sauce, not soy — depth without soybeans

hero.alt: Tom kha gai with coconut broth, mushrooms, and cilantro
hero.caption: tom kha gai — coconut, lime

healthFacts: Naturally soy-free — fish sauce is the umami source.
Galangal (related to ginger) has gingerol-like anti-inflammatory compounds.
Lemongrass essential oils have antimicrobial activity in vitro studies.

steps.title: Bruise the aromatics
steps.detail: Smash lemongrass, slice galangal in coins, tear lime leaves. Bruising releases volatile oils.
steps.tip: Frozen galangal/leaves work fine — keep a stash.

steps.title: Steep the broth
steps.detail: Pour coconut milk and stock into a pot. Add aromatics. Bring to a gentle simmer; steep 8 min.
steps.tip: Don't boil coconut milk hard — it splits.

steps.title: Cook chicken and mushrooms
steps.detail: Add chicken and mushrooms; simmer 8 min until chicken is just cooked through. Stir in fish sauce.
steps.tip: Slice chicken thin against the grain — cooks fast and stays tender.

steps.title: Lime and herbs
steps.detail: Off heat, stir in lime juice. Top with cilantro and sliced bird's eye chili. Eat hot — diners pluck out aromatics as they go.
steps.tip: Lime always at the end — heat destroys its brightness.

media.alt: Tom kha bowl with cilantro and chili
media.caption: lime and herbs
